hero.title: Request a free interpretation with synthetic data.
hero.description: Describe your real flow in a simple way and receive a synthetic interpretation and a full report later, directly in your email.
hero.securityNote: No sensitive data in the first contact.

orderId.name: order_id
orderId.role: Identify each order in the flow

form.title: Tell us a bit about the flow
form.description: You do not need to send a real dataset. Describe the flow, the expected signals and the fields that would help the simulation.
form.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the flow, the main steps and what happens today.
form.affectedContextPlaceholder: Ex.: Pix + Mobile, WhatsApp + new customers
form.simulationGoalPlaceholder: What decision do you expect this reading to support? Ex.: understanding whether it is worth investigating Pix approval on mobile before requesting real data.
form.signalsTitle: Which signals would you like to simulate?
form.signalsDescription: Select the signals that make sense for your flow.
form.fieldsDescription: List the main fields that help explain the behavior of the flow and briefly describe the role of each one.
form.fieldRolePlaceholder: Ex.: Identify each order in the flow
form.emailSectionTitle: Where should we send the report?
form.syntheticDisclaimer: This request uses synthetic data generated by Ohrly. We do not analyze or diagnose your real operation in this first contact.
form.successMessage: Request sent successfully. We will prepare the synthetic interpretation and send the report by email.
form.errorMessage: We could not send your request right now. Please try again in a few moments.

sidePanel.title: What you will receive

syntheticReading.title: Synthetic flow reading
syntheticReading.description: A clear analysis of the simulated behavior of your flow.

observableSignals.title: Observable signals and affected context
observableSignals.description: The most relevant signals and the contexts that most influence the result.

operationalState.title: Operational state hypothesis
operationalState.description: An objective hypothesis about the operational state based on the signals.

emailReport.title: Report sent by email
emailReport.description: A complete report ready to share with your team.

sidePanel.idealTitle: Ideal to start without a real dataset

idealItems.receiveByEmail: You receive the reading by email

outputPreview.label: Output example
outputPreview.title: Checkout with slow approval
outputPreview.description: The simulation suggests operational attention in Pix + Mobile, with increasing recovery pressure.

process.closing: Our goal is to make the problem legible before asking for real data.

title.before: Ohrly helps resilient companies identify degradations that

hero.description: Ohrly helps teams understand when critical flows keep running but start losing efficiency, consistency, or recovery capacity before the impact becomes explicit.

solutions.title: Choose the path closest to your operation.

ecommerce.title: Ohrly for E-commerce
ecommerce.description: Detect when checkout, payment, delivery, or cart recovery start losing efficiency before conversion drop becomes obvious.

bots.title: Ohrly for Bots & Agents
bots.description: Detect when bots and agents keep responding but start transferring more to humans, resolving less, or accumulating unresolved sessions.

howItWorks.title: How Ohrly works

context.title: Context
context.description: Signals are enriched with operational context.

persistence.title: Persistence
persistence.description: Contextual data is stored for longitudinal analysis.

reading.title: Reading
reading.description: Ohrly computes the RPI behavior reading.

howItWorks.description: From raw behavior to a single reading that shows when performance is diverging from expected patterns.

reports.title: Public Reports
reports.description: Browse published synthetic interpretations and examples.

finalCta.title: Start with the route that fits your operation.
finalCta.description: Explore a focused solution first, then use the Laboratory to see how Ohrly would read a similar flow with synthetic data.

hero.title: See a ready-made reading before configuring anything.
hero.description: Choose a flow example, make a simple adjustment and see how Ohrly interprets noise and bottlenecks before the impact becomes obvious.
hero.securityNote: No integration. No real data in the first contact.

reading.summaryPrefix: The flow still works, but shows
reading.rpiHelp: Index that estimates how much pressure the flow is accumulating to return to expected behavior.

checkout.title: Checkout with slow approval
checkout.shortDescription: Payment keeps working, but confirmation time starts to increase.
checkout.scenario: persistent operational noise in the Pix + Mobile context
checkout.stateHelp: When a flow still works but already shows persistent signs of worsening that deserve attention.
checkout.whyItMatters: When this kind of signal persists, the operation can start accumulating retries, abandonment, rework or efficiency loss before any explicit incident.

bot.title: Bot with growing handoff
bot.scenario: increasing handoff pressure on WhatsApp for new customers
bot.stateHelp: When the automated flow still responds, but starts requiring more human support persistently.
bot.whyItMatters: This signal can increase support costs, pressure human queues and hide efficiency loss in the automated flow before any formal incident.

delivery.title: Delivery with slow recovery
delivery.shortDescription: Orders are still delivered, but the flow takes longer to return to normal.
delivery.scenario: slower recovery among mid-size sellers in the Northeast
delivery.stateHelp: When the flow keeps running, but spends too much time outside expected behavior.
delivery.whyItMatters: Delays that look isolated can start consuming the operation's recovery window, increasing support and reducing predictability for the customer.

billing.title: Billing with recurring failures
billing.scenario: recurring recovery failures in mid-market Pro plan accounts
billing.stateHelp: When recovery still works, but starts depending on more attempts and loses predictability.
billing.whyItMatters: Recurring billing failures may look small at first, but they accumulate revenue risk, manual effort and loss of trust in the recovery process.

comparison.title: Ohrly does not start with infrastructure. It starts with the flow.
comparison.closing: The question is not only whether the system is up. It is whether the flow is still working well for the business.

reports.title: Explore published interpretations.
reports.description: See requests transformed into synthetic readings and understand how different digital flows can generate noise, bottlenecks and efficiency loss before the impact becomes explicit.
reports.searchPlaceholder: Search by flow, domain or signal...

retencaoBoletoConversacional.title: Retention in conversational boleto flow
retencaoBoletoConversacional.summary: The retention drop did not appear in isolation. It came together with boleto failures, human handoff, longer support time and complaints.

checkoutAprovacaoLenta.title: Checkout with slow approval
checkoutAprovacaoLenta.summary: Payment keeps working, but the time to confirmation starts increasing in the Pix + Mobile context.

timeline.criticalWindow: Critical window: late Feb – early Mar

cta.title: Test Ohrly with your own flow
cta.description: See how synthetic or real data can become a clear, connected and actionable reading.
cta.button: Request an interpretation with synthetic data

retencao-boleto-conversacional.title: Retention in conversational boleto flow
retencao-boleto-conversacional.summary: The retention drop did not appear in isolation. In the synthetic scenario, it came together with boleto failures, human handoff, longer support time and complaints.
retencao-boleto-conversacional.disclaimer: This reading uses synthetic data and is demonstrative. It shows how Ohrly would interpret a similar flow if it had access to real historical data.
retencao-boleto-conversacional.oneSentence: The flow kept working, but several signals were under pressure at the same time: retention, boleto failure, human handoff, support time, total resolution and complaints.
retencao-boleto-conversacional.whyItMatters: When retention drops together with boleto failures, handoff and complaints, the problem stops looking like just a conversion metric. It starts looking like an operational bottleneck spreading through the flow.

evidence.simultaneousSignals: signals under pressure simultaneously in the main window

requested.objective: Understand whether retention variations could be associated with boleto failures, human handoff, support time, final resolution and complaints.

retencao-boleto-conversacional.interpretation: Ohrly created a synthetic base to represent the described flow, simulated behavioral signals and analyzed whether they appeared in isolation or connected over time.

checkout-aprovacao-lenta.title: Checkout with slow approval
checkout-aprovacao-lenta.summary: Payment keeps working, but time to confirmation starts increasing in the Pix + Mobile context.
checkout-aprovacao-lenta.disclaimer: This reading uses synthetic data and is demonstrative. It shows how Ohrly would interpret a similar flow if it had access to real historical data.
checkout-aprovacao-lenta.oneSentence: The flow still completes payments, but time to approval remained under pressure long enough to stop looking like a normal fluctuation.
checkout-aprovacao-lenta.whyItMatters: When approval starts taking longer in a specific context, the operation can accumulate retries, abandonment and efficiency loss before any explicit incident.

evidence.persistentSignal: of persistent signal in the synthetic scenario

requested.objective: Simulate whether a gradual increase in time to approval could indicate operational noise before an explicit conversion drop.

checkout-aprovacao-lenta.interpretation: Ohrly generated a synthetic scenario for the checkout flow, simulated slower approval in Pix + Mobile and analyzed persistence, recovery pressure and associated signals.

checkout.name: Checkout and payment
checkout.description: From checkout start to order confirmation.

delivery.name: Delivery
delivery.description: From approved order to completed delivery.

cart.name: Cart
cart.description: From cart creation to purchase attempt.

billing.name: Billing
billing.description: From generated charge to recovery or account at risk.

delivery.title: Delivery with slow recovery
delivery.summary: Orders are still being delivered, but the flow takes longer to return to expected behavior.
delivery.apmSignal: Active jobs, APIs responding, and errors within tolerable levels.
delivery.ohrlyReading: The delivery journey is requiring more recovery time and creating operational queues before becoming a massive complaint issue.

cart.title: Cart with growing abandonment
cart.summary: The cart is still being created, but more customers give up before starting payment.
cart.apmSignal: Frontend loading, API without downtime, and events being recorded.
cart.ohrlyReading: User behavior changed: more sessions stop before payment, especially in a specific context.

billing.title: Billing with unstable recovery
billing.summary: Charges are still running, but recovery starts depending on more attempts.
billing.apmSignal: Gateway available, queues consumed, and failures without a critical spike.
billing.ohrlyReading: Revenue recovery is becoming more fragile: the same result requires more attempts and more time.

checkout_more_retries.title: Checkout with growing retries
checkout_more_retries.summary: Payment is still working, but more orders require new attempts before confirmation.
checkout_more_retries.apmSignal: Gateway online, acceptable error rate, and latency without a critical alert.
checkout_more_retries.ohrlyReading: The flow is becoming more expensive to complete the same sale: there is more effort before confirmation.

checkout_mobile_drop.title: Checkout with mobile drop
checkout_mobile_drop.summary: The flow remains active, but mobile completion starts drifting away from expected behavior.
checkout_mobile_drop.apmSignal: Application available, routes responding, and checkout still processing orders.
checkout_mobile_drop.ohrlyReading: The issue is not downtime: it is efficiency loss concentrated on mobile before appearing as a general drop.

checkout_slow_approval.title: Checkout with slow approval
checkout_slow_approval.summary: Payment is still working, but time to approval increased for a few days and retries started appearing.
checkout_slow_approval.apmSignal: Checkout online, few errors, and latency still below the critical threshold.
checkout_slow_approval.ohrlyReading: The flow is losing efficiency: approval takes longer, requires more attempts, and exposes revenue before the incident becomes obvious.

hero.title: Your e-commerce does not need to go down to start losing money.
hero.description: Checkout, payment, delivery, and cart recovery may keep working while already accumulating delays, retries, abandonment, and efficiency loss.

apm.label: APM shows health
apm.text: Your system may be healthy and still generate operational losses.

ohrly.label: Ohrly interprets it
ohrly.text: Even a healthy system may be operating below its potential.

comparison.title: Identify relevant signals that indicate something may be breaking.
comparison.description: Ohrly does not replace APM or BI. It connects both: showing when technical behavior starts pressuring operational results.

apm.title: APM answers
apm.description: Is the service available? Are there errors? Did latency cross the threshold?

bi.title: BI answers
bi.description: Did conversion drop? Did revenue change? Did the final result get worse?

ohrly.title: Ohrly answers
ohrly.description: Did the flow start losing efficiency before the impact became obvious?

symptoms.title: The store is still selling. But the flow may already be getting worse.
symptoms.description: The point is not only detecting explicit failures. It is noticing when small, persistent, connected signals indicate operational efficiency loss.

slowCheckout.title: Slower checkout
slowCheckout.apm: Latency still within the technical limit.
slowCheckout.ohrly: Time to approval increased and started creating friction in the purchase journey.

moreRetries.title: More retries
moreRetries.apm: Gateway available and transactions still being processed.
moreRetries.ohrly: The same sale now requires more attempts, increasing cost and abandonment risk.

moreAbandonment.title: More abandonment
moreAbandonment.apm: Events keep arriving and the application is not down.
moreAbandonment.ohrly: Customers are stopping before completion in a specific context, such as mobile or Pix.

fragileDelivery.title: More fragile delivery
fragileDelivery.apm: Jobs, queues, and integrations may look healthy in isolation.
fragileDelivery.ohrly: The flow takes longer to recover and starts accumulating operational pressure.

simulator.title: Simulate a flow similar to yours
simulator.description: Instead of choosing loose metrics, you simulate a journey, a degradation scenario, and the accumulated operational pressure.

steps.four: Receive a reading, not just a chart

method.title: Ohrly interprets the flow trajectory, not just component health.

businessFlow.title: Starts from the business flow
businessFlow.description: It does not start from a service or endpoint. It starts from the journey that needs to generate results.

intermediateSignals.title: Reads intermediate signals
intermediateSignals.description: Retries, delays, abandonment, recovery, and partial completion before the clear incident.

contextComparison.title: Compares by context
contextComparison.description: Mobile, Pix, region, plan, seller, or channel may have different expected behaviors.

persistencePropagation.title: Measures persistence and propagation
persistencePropagation.description: Differentiates isolated spikes from sustained degradation that starts spreading impact.

decisionTranslation.title: Translates into decision
decisionTranslation.description: Shows flow state, recovery pressure, exposed value, and operational priority.

finalCta.title: Test with a flow similar to yours
finalCta.description: Ideal for teams that already monitor systems but still need to understand when a critical journey started losing operational efficiency.

moreFallback.title: More fallback
moreFallback.description: The bot relies more on generic or non-resolving responses.

longerResolution.title: Longer resolution time
longerResolution.description: Each interaction takes longer to complete.

unresolvedSessions.title: More sessions without outcome
unresolvedSessions.description: More conversations end without effective resolution.

expectedFlow.title: Define the expected flow
expectedFlow.description: We map what the bot or agent should deliver under normal conditions.

behaviorSignals.title: Observe behavioral signals
behaviorSignals.description: We capture real behavioral signals from conversations over time.

contextComparison.title: Compare by context
contextComparison.description: We compare against recent history and similar contexts.

persistenceMagnitudePropagation.title: Measure persistence, magnitude, and propagation
persistenceMagnitudePropagation.description: We evaluate whether the change is recurring, relevant, and spreading to other segments.

actionableReading.title: Generate an actionable reading
actionableReading.description: We deliver RPI, key signals, and what is worth investigating or acting on.

apm.title: APM sees:
apm.subtitle: service and error
apm.description: Focuses on technical health and availability.

bi.title: BI sees:
bi.subtitle: result
bi.description: Focuses on what was delivered at the end of the journey.

ohrly.title: Ohrly sees:
ohrly.subtitle: the flow losing efficiency
ohrly.description: Focuses on what changes before the result gets worse.

hero.badge: Drift analysis for bots and agents
hero.title: Your bot may be degrading before handoff explodes.
hero.description: Bots and agents may keep responding while already resolving less, transferring more to humans, and accumulating sessions without outcome.
hero.privacyNote: No integration. No real data on the first contact.

dashboard.recoveryDescription: The bot is holding up, but with more effort.

insights.handoff: Handoff increasing over the last 2 weeks

symptomsSection.title: The bot did not go down. But something has already changed.

exampleReading.title: See a ready Bot Drift reading

startWithoutIntegration.title: Start without integration
startWithoutIntegration.description: A simple dataset exported from your system is enough.
startWithoutIntegration.privacyNote: CSV or Parquet. No sensitive PII on the first contact.

finalCta.title: Anticipate problems. Act before handoff explodes.
finalCta.description: Request a synthetic analysis of your flow and receive a ready reading with the main signals.

footer.description: Ohrly helps teams interpret the behavior of critical digital flows, identifying when they start losing consistency before degradation becomes an obvious incident, rework, or business impact.

status.title: In validation
status.description: Built from real operational pain and currently evolving through prototype, simulations, and field feedback.

location.title: Recife, Brazil
location.description: Created from the Porto Digital ecosystem, with a focus on digital operations and behavioral observability.

portoDigital.title: Pre-incubated at Porto Digital
portoDigital.description: Ohrly is part of the Porto Digital pre-incubation program, 2026.1 cohort, where the product is being structured, validated, and refined with support from the innovation ecosystem.
portoDigital.note: This stage strengthens the product discovery process, market validation, and the evolution of Ohrly as a behavioral observability solution for critical digital flows.

footer.copyright: Behavioral observability for critical digital flows.
